checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is present in the source document. No fabricated sections, headings, or body text were found. The document title and image alt text are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.

The contact information for Betsy Haywood, including the address (1701 Mail Service Center), phone number ((919) 707-0013), and email address (betsy.haywood@ncwildlife.org), was verified against the source and is accurate.
All pages and sections from the source document are represented in the output, including the fiscal note review on page 1 and the rule appendix on page 2.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content of the paragraphs and rule items is preserved. No significant portions of sections were found to be missing or truncated.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are several major defects in the rule text (Appendix 1) where the output misrepresents the status of tracked changes (additions vs. deletions), which is critical in a legal rule-making document. In item (a)(1), the word 'Onslow-Pender' is underlined (added) in the source but marked with strikethrough (deleted) in the output. In item (a)(2), the text 'between a point 100 yards upstream... trestle;' is plain text in the source but is marked with strikethrough in the output. Also in (a)(2), the words 'near River' are processed as 'River, near' and entirely struck through, whereas only 'River' is struck in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1. '~~east of the Pender~~ Onslow-Pender County line' (Note: Onslow-Pender is underlined)
2. 'between a point 100 yards upstream, and 300 yards downstream, of the Seaboard Coast Line Railroad trestle;'
3. 'near ~~River~~ in the City'
checks.b_evidence_output:
1. '"text": "east of the Pender Onslow-Pender", ... "strikethrough": true'
2. '"text": "between a point 100 yards upstream... trestle; ...", ... "strikethrough": true'
3. '"text": "River, near", ... "strikethrough": true'

checks.a_analysis: The content appears in the output the same number of times as it appears in the source PDF. Some similar financial information is repeated in both the narrative text and the Local Impact Analysis section of the original document, which the output reflects accurately.

The document structure misinterprets the relationship between the 'Authority' field and the subsequent narrative body text. The output incorrectly nests all narrative paragraphs, impact analysis sections, and the footnote from the first page inside the 'Authority' subsection. In the source, the narrative text is a separate block following the header fields, not part of the Authority citation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The narrative text starting with 'Onslow County applied for rulemaking...' follows the 'Authority: G.S. 75A-3: 75A-15' line as a separate conceptual section.
checks.b_evidence_output: The elements list for sections[0].subsections[2] ('Authority:') includes the narrative paragraphs starting at element index 1 ('Onslow County applied for rulemaking...') through index 7 ('*total compensation').

checks.a_analysis: No tables are present in the source document.

The overall reading order follows the top-to-bottom flow of the source document correctly. While there is a nesting issue identified in S2, the sequential order of content elements remains faithful to the source and logical for navigation.

The ordered list on page 2 (Regulated Areas) is converted inconsistently. Items (1), (2), and (3) have had their list markers stripped from the text content, while both items labeled (4) (the strikethrough and underlined items) retain the '(4)' marker in their text content. This results in inconsistent navigation and visual representation.
checks.b_evidence_source: (1) Old Settler's Beach..., (2) New River..., (3) Wheeler's Point..., (4) Swansboro Harbor..., (4) the waters of Queens Creek...
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Old Settler's Beach...", "text": "New River...", "text": "Wheeler's Point...", "text": "(4) Swansboro Harbor...", "text": "(4) the waters of Queens Creek..."

checks.a_analysis: The email link for Betsy Haywood is correctly identified and formatted as a functional mailto link. No other links are present or incorrectly injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and logical flow of the original PDF document. The header information (Contact, Impact, Authority) is correctly placed before the main body text, and the Appendix section follows at the end, consistent with the source PDF's structure.
There are no tables in the source PDF document. The key-value pairs in the Contact and Impact sections are rendered as plain text, which is an acceptable representation of the original layout.
The North Carolina Wildlife Resources Commission logo present at the top of the source PDF is correctly rendered as an image in the HTML output.
There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML output. Each paragraph, list item, and section header appears once, mirroring the original document's content structure.
